title: Golden Gate Assembly Protocol for NEB Golden Gate Assembly Mix
author: Alba Balletbó
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7kfhktn
materials: For each assembly, thaw a 50 µL tube of NEB 10-beta competent E. coli cells on ice for 5–10 minutes.
Add 950 µL of room temperature NEB 10-beta/Stable Outgrowth Medium. Incubate at 37ºC for 60 minutes, shaking vigorously (250 rpm) or using a rotation device.

steps.title: Set up 25 µl assembly reactions as follows
steps.instruction: Set up 25 µl assembly reactions as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting up and down 4 times
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting up and down 4 times.

steps.title: Briefly microcentrifuge (1 second) to bring...
steps.instruction: Briefly microcentrifuge (1 second) to bring material to the bottom of tube.

steps.title: Transfer to thermocycler and program as follows
steps.instruction: Transfer to thermocycler and program as follows: (5 min 37ºC → 5 min 16ºC) x 30 cycles followed by 5 minutes 60ºC. If reactions are done overnight, add a 4ºC terminal hold to the protocol, but repeat the final 5 minutes 60ºC step the next day before the transformations.

steps.title: Add 2 µL of the assembly...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of the assembly reaction; gently mix by flicking the tube 4-5 times.

steps.title: Warm LB agar plates containing chloramphenicol...
steps.instruction: Warm LB agar plates containing chloramphenicol (for pGGA) at 37ºC for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking...
steps.instruction: Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking the tube and inverting, then spread 100 µL outgrowth onto each plate.

steps.title: Incubate the plates overnight at 37ºC,...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plates overnight at 37ºC, or 24 hours at 30ºC, or 48 hours at 25ºC.
